titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Department of Athletics, Yale University, Records Documenting Polo
Department of Athletics, Yale University, Records Documenting Polo
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 12, 2020 at 07:01
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Department of Athletics, Yale University, records documenting polo
did.abstract: The records consist of the files of Daniel J. Wallace, Jr. as coach, scrapbooks, correspondence, and photographs documenting polo at Yale. Also included is a research paper on the sport by Alexandra Redding, Class of 2002.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 38-B (YRG 38-B), Records documenting specific sports at Yale.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Polo scrapbooks are available on microfilm from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, at cost. Order no. HM185. 1984. 1215 frames on 1 reel, 35mm.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The records were transferred by the Department of Athletics and private donors, 2001-2005.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is partially restricted. See inventory for details. The scrapbooks have been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 185 instead of the originals.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Patrons must use HM FILM 185 instead of the original scrapbooks.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Department of Athletics, Yale University, Records Documenting Polo (RU 908), Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of the files of Daniel J. Wallace, Jr. as coach, scrapbooks, correspondence, and photographs documenting polo at Yale. Also included is a research paper on the sport by Alexandra Redding, Class of 2002.

corpname.part: Yale University. Dept. of Athletics, Physical Education, and Recreation

did.unittitle: Records of Daniel J. Wallace, Jr., as coach of the Yale polo team
Surveys of other college riding programs obtained by Wallace
Letter to J.C. Rathborne from Yale Polo
Newspaper clippings, originals and photocopies, on Yale Polo. Those from the New Haven Register were carried out under Wallace, who worked there during the time he coached Yale Polo
Programs from other college polo programs (Cornell) and from polo clubs where Wallace belonged (Pinehurst)
United States Polo Association Polo Newsletters
USPA "25thWomen's National Intercollegiate Polo Tournament." Wallace is featured as the founder of women's intercollegiate polo. Yale Polo history is mentioned
Articles on American polo history written by Wallace in "Polo" magazine
New Haven Register articles on Yale Polo
Article and correspondence on Yale Polo's record
Proposed constitution. Yale Polo Association letter
Correspondence regarding the Yale Polo Association and other
Articles on the history of Yale Polo. Season record sheets 1947-1970
Yale Polo Association letters. 1942 Yale Polo Association letter concerning the end of polo due to World War Two. 1970s letters and memo regarding the Yale Polo program and its reorganization
Universityof Connecticutarticle on "The Comeback of the Horse." Yale Polo alumni fundraising response
Letters regarding the Yale Polo capital fund campaign
Yale Polo season schedules, reports. Letters.
Copies of printed material from binder
"The Evolution of Polo in America during the 20th Century,"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Redding, Alexandra. "The Evolution of Polo in America during the 20th Century"

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Scrapbooks are available on microfilm. Patrons must use HM FILM 185 instead of the original scrapbooks.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 185 instead of the originals.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 185 instead of the originals.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 185 instead of the originals.
